title: Why Humans Sing
puzzle: Why do humans in nearly every culture turn language into melody?
insight: Singing combines rhythm, emotion, and shared timing, which makes it powerful for bonding groups, expressing feeling, and strengthening memory.
key_ideas: Songs are often social, not only individual.
Melody makes words easier to remember.
Group singing can create strong feelings of unity.
mind_blower: A song can synchronize bodies, emotion, and attention faster than a speech can.
next_curiosity: If singing bonds groups so effectively, what does that reveal about cooperation?
narration_text: Why do humans in nearly every culture turn language into melody? Singing combines rhythm, emotion, and shared timing, which makes it powerful for bonding groups, expressing feeling, and strengthening memory. Songs are often social, not only individual. Melody makes words easier to remember. Group singing can create strong feelings of unity. A song can synchronize bodies, emotion, and attention faster than a speech can. If singing bonds groups so effectively, what does that reveal about cooperation?
